title: WASHED UP
creator: Ani Acopian (Atlas Acopian) & Cody Guilfoyle
award_or_showcase_provenance: Dronie Category winner, New York City Drone Film Festival (NYCDFF), 2019 -- verified this session via WebFetch of https://www.nycdronefilmfestival.com/winners, which lists the entry as 'WASHED UP by ANI ACOPIAN & CODY GUILFOYLE' under the 2019 Dronie Category
notes: SUBGENRE-DIVERSIFYING EXEMPLAR (single-take 'dronie' / short-form novelty format, distinct from this pack's landscape-compilation and FPV-piloting exemplars) -- built to the same rigor/honesty standard as bbc-earth-aerial.json's original worked example. `source_url`/`title`/`creator`/`duration` were independently verified this session via Vimeo's own oEmbed endpoint (https://vimeo.com/api/oembed.json?url=https://vimeo.com/238460429), which returned title='WASHED UP', author_name='Atlas Acopian', author_url='https://vimeo.com/atlasacopian', duration=15 (seconds), description='Taken in Reynisfjara, Iceland on my DJI Mavic Pro. The water was 43 degrees.', upload_date='2017-10-16 15:16:02' -- a direct, primary-source confirmation of the exact title, runtime, and platform-registered author for this specific upload. A direct WebFetch of the video's own vimeo.com/238460429 page was blocked by a Vimeo bot/security-verification interstitial in this session (returned only a 'needs to review the security of your connection' screen, no video metadata), so the oEmbed endpoint -- not the rendered page -- is this session's actual verification source for those four fields; this is disclosed rather than papered over. `creator` is recorded as 'Ani Acopian (Atlas Acopian) & Cody Guilfoyle' rather than oEmbed's author_name alone ('Atlas Acopian'): a WebFetch of the New York City Drone Film Festival's own winners page (https://www.nycdronefilmfestival.com/winners) independently corroborates this, listing the 2019 Dronie Category entry as 'WASHED UP by ANI ACOPIAN & CODY GUILFOYLE' -- i.e. two independent primary sources (Vimeo's own oEmbed account handle 'atlasacopian', and the festival's own listing 'ANI ACOPIAN') agree this is the same person under two name forms, and the festival listing additionally confirms Cody Guilfoyle as co-creator (matching a WebSearch finding that Guilfoyle piloted the drone while Acopian lay in the surf). `award_or_showcase_provenance` is a real, directly-sourced juried-contest win (not inferred or elimination-based), matching this pack's precedent for contest-verified exemplars (e.g. ny-drone-film-festival.json's Landscape-category win, same festival). No Creative Commons marking, license field, or CC badge was present anywhere in the oEmbed response (its full key set was enumerated this session: type, version, provider_name, provider_url, title, author_name, author_url, is_plus, account_type, html, width, height, duration, description, thumbnail_url/dimensions, upload_date, video_id, uri -- no 'license' key) -- per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `license_category` is set to 'all-rights-reserved', so `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint, and no live playback of this specific video was performed in this session (the Vimeo page itself was inaccessible behind the security interstitial noted above). Calibration reasoning: sharpness=0.68 and exposure=0.66 are set below this pack's modern 1-inch-sensor/5.4K-era exemplars (e.g. instagram-elisacappelletti-monument-valley.json's Mavic Air 2S at 0.74/0.73) because the oEmbed-confirmed camera is a first-generation DJI Mavic Pro (2016, 12.35MP, 1/2.3" sensor, no confirmed HDR/log profile) shot outdoors at an exposed black-sand beach under what press/thumbnail context suggests is flat, overcast Icelandic coastal light -- a genuinely lower technical sensor/lighting baseline than this pack's newer-hardware exemplars, not a genre-based markdown. motion_smoothness=0.72 is set slightly higher than sharpness/exposure, in line with this pack's existing convention (e.g. drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json's documented practice) of crediting motion quality when a clip's core creative premise depends on it: a 'dronie' is definitionally a single continuous ascending/receding gimbal-stabilized move away from the subject, so smooth motion is both the entire technical challenge and the entire creative payoff of the format, and it was this movement quality -- not sharpness or exposure -- that the NYCDFF's own Dronie category was judging. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-estimated: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.68, exposure=0.66, motion_smoothness=0.72, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights(weights_version='default-v1')` (equal 1/3 weighting on sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness, composition weight fixed at 0.0) via `.venv/bin/python -c "..."` in this session, returning 0.6866666666666666 (rounded to 0.6867 here) -- so the composite arithmetic is genuine and reproducible even though its three inputs are estimates rather than measurements.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Not live-played in this session (the Vimeo watch page was blocked by a bot/security-verification interstitial, so only the oEmbed metadata and description text were available). Per that description ('Taken in Reynisfjara, Iceland on my DJI Mavic Pro') and the format's own definition, a 'dronie' is a single continuous, uncut drone-selfie take -- the subject (Acopian, lying in the surf as waves wash over him) stays in frame while the drone ascends/pulls back in one unbroken shot for the full 15-second oEmbed-confirmed duration, with no edits or transitions. estimated_cut_count=0 and avg_shot_length_seconds=15.0 (equal to the full clip runtime) follow directly from this text-provenance understanding of the format, not from having watched the clip play out.
